Caption: Schematic illustration of semi-batch leaching reactor.
Volume evolution in a semi-batch reactor (V 0 = Initial solution volume; V j = Volume before sampling; V ja = Volume after sampling).

Effect of acid feeding operation on the leaching of (a) lithium and (b) cobalt; (c) pH profile and protons consumption and (d) mole ratio of Co/Li extracted in different operations described in Table 2: batch (B1) with 1.5 M HCl added initially (red triangle); batch (B2) with 1 M HCl added initially (black circles); semi-batch (S1) with 0.2 M HCl added initially and continuously to maintain pH at 0.88 (blue triangles) (other conditions: S/L ratio 50 g/L; 0.5 M H2O2; 25 °C). (For interpretation of the references to colour in this figure legend, the reader is referred to the web version of this article.)

Effect of initial H2O2 concentration of 0 M (S2, triangles), 0.25 M (S3, circles), 0.5 M (S1, diamonds) and 1 M (S4, squares) in a semi-batch reactor on leaching of lithium (a), cobalt (b) and consumption of H+ (c) as a function of time, and mole ratio of H+/Co2+ after 2 h (d) (other conditions: S/L ratio 50 g/L; 0.2 M HCl; 25 °C, total time 2 h).

Effect of initial HCl concentration of 0.2 M (S4, diamonds), 0.1 M (S5, circles), and 0.01 M (S6, triangles) in a semi-batch reactor on leaching of lithium (a), cobalt (b) and consumption of H+ (c) as a function of time over 1 h, and mole ratio of H+/Co2+ after 2 h (d) (other conditions: S/L ratio 50 g/L; 1 M H2O2; 25 °C).

Effect of double addition of HCl (0.1 M) and H2O2 (1, 0.5 or 0.25 M) in a semi-batch reactor on leaching of lithium (a), cobalt (b) and consumption of protons and H2O2 (c) as a function of time over 2 h and mole ratio of H+/Co2+ (d) after 2 h (other conditions: S/L ratio 50 g/L; pH 1.15, 25 °C, 2 h).
Reactant consumption and metal percentage extracted based on the reactor mode at 30 min.
